title: Exponential expressions word problems (numerical)
description: Write exponential expressions that describe a quantity in a real-world context.

assessment_items.question_content:
Karim sent a chain email to $10$ of his friends. The number of people who got the email increases by a factor of $1.4$ every week.

**Which expression gives the number of people who got the email after $6$ weeks?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
After $0$ weeks (i.e., when the email was sent), the number of people who got the email was $10$.

After $1$ week, that number was multiplied by $1.4$, so it was $10\cdot 1.4$.

After $2$ weeks, the number was again multiplied by $1.4$, so it was $10\cdot 1.4\cdot 1.4=10\cdot 1.4^2$.

We can keep doing this until we get to $6$ weeks.

In conclusion, this is an expression for the number of people who got the email after $6$ weeks:

$10\cdot 1.4^6$

assessment_items.question_content:
The number of bacteria in a petri dish is multiplied by a factor of $1.2$ every hour. There were initially $500$ bacteria.

**Which expression gives the number of bacteria after $3$ hours?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
After $0$ hours (i.e., at the initial time), the number of bacteria was $500$.

After $1$ hour, that number was multiplied by $1.2$, so it was $500\cdot 1.2$.

After $2$ hours, the number was again multiplied by $1.2$, so it was $500\cdot 1.2\cdot 1.2=500\cdot 1.2^2$.

We can keep doing this until we get to $3$ hours.

In conclusion, this is an expression for the number of bacteria after $3$ hours:

$500\cdot 1.2\cdot 1.2\cdot 1.2$

assessment_items.question_content:
Shota invests $\$1000$ in a certificate of deposit that earns interest. The investment's value is multiplied by $1.02$ each year.

**Which expression gives the investment's value after $5$ years?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
After $0$ years (i.e., at the time of investment), the investment's value was $1000$ dollars.

After $1$ year, that value was multiplied by $1.02$, so it was $1000\cdot 1.02$.

After $2$ years, the value was again multiplied by $1.02$, so it was $1000\cdot 1.02\cdot 1.02$, which is $1000\cdot 1.02^2$.

We can keep doing this until we get to $5$ years.

In conclusion, this is an expression for the investment's value after $5$ years:

$1000\cdot 1.02^5$

assessment_items.question_content:
Javier bought a painting for $\$150$. Each year, the painting's value increases by a factor of $1.15$.

**Which expression gives the painting's value after $7$ years?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
After $0$ years (i.e., at the time when Javier bought the painting), the painting's value was $150$ dollars.

After $1$ year, that number was multiplied by $1.15$, so it was $150\cdot 1.15$.

After $2$ years, the number was again multiplied by $1.15$, so it was $150\cdot 1.15\cdot 1.15$, which is $150\cdot 1.15^2$.

We can keep doing this until we get to $7$ years.

In conclusion, this is an expression for the painting's value after $7$ years:

$150\cdot 1.15^7$

assessment_items.question_content:
When the Dragons have lost their most recent game, fans buy $200$ tickets. For each consecutive win the Dragons have, the number of tickets fans buy increases by a factor of $1.1$.

**Which expression gives the number of tickets fans buy after $4$ consecutive wins?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
After $0$ consecutive wins (i.e., the most recent game when the Dragons lost), fans buy $200$ tickets.

After $1$ win, that number is multiplied by $1.1$, so it is $200\cdot 1.1$.

After $2$ consecutive wins, the number is again multiplied by $1.1$, so it is $200\cdot 1.1\cdot 1.1$, which is $200\cdot 1.1^2$.

We can keep doing this until we get to $4$ consecutive wins.

In conclusion, this is an expression for the number of tickets fans buy after $4$ consecutive wins:

$200\cdot 1.1\cdot 1.1\cdot 1.1\cdot 1.1$

assessment_items.question_content:
The number of nano-related patents that are granted in the US increases by a factor of $1.2$ every year. In $1991$, there were $60$ nano-related patents.

**Which expression gives the number of patents in $1998$?**

[[☃ radio 3]]
hints:
After $0$ years (i.e., in $1991$), there were $60$ patents.

After $1$ year, that number was multiplied by $1.2$, so it was $60\cdot 1.2$.

After $2$ years, the number was again multiplied by $1.2$, so it was $60\cdot 1.2\cdot 1.2$, which is $60\cdot 1.2^2$.

We can keep doing this until we get to $7$ years after $1991$, which is $1998$.

In conclusion, this is an expression for the number of nano-related patents in $1998$:

$60\cdot 1.2^7$

assessment_items.question_content:
On the day a certain celebrity proposes marriage, $8$ people know about it. Each day afterward, the number of people who know grows by $50\%$.

**Which expression gives the number of people who know about the proposal after $4$ days?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints: Growth at a rate of $50\%$ means the number of people keeps its $100\%$ and adds $50\%$ more. So each day, the number is multiplied by $100\%+50\%$, which is the same as a factor of $(1+0.5)$.

After $0$ days (i.e., the time of proposal), the number of people who know about the proposal is $8$.

After $1$ day, that number is multiplied by $1+0.5$, so it is $8\cdot (1+0.5)$.

After $2$ days, the number is again multiplied by $1+0.5$, so it is $8\cdot (1+0.5)\cdot (1+0.5)$, which is $8\cdot(1+0.5)^2$.

We can keep doing this until we get to $4$ days.

In conclusion, this is an expression for the number of people who know about the proposal after $4$ days:

$8\cdot(1+0.5)\cdot(1+0.5)\cdot(1+0.5)\cdot(1+0.5)$

assessment_items.question_content:
The town of Springfield, USA has a population of $9900$ people. The population is growing at a rate of $2.3\%$ each year.

**Which expression gives the town's population $5$ years from now?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Growth at a rate of $2.3\%$ means the population keeps its $100\%$ and adds $2.3\%$ more. So each year, the population size is multiplied by $100\%+2.3\%$, which is the same as a factor of $(1+0.023)$.

After $0$ years (i.e., now), the population is $9900$ people.

After $1$ year, that number is multiplied by $1+0.023$, so it is $9900\cdot (1+0.023)$.

After $2$ years, the number is again multiplied by $1+0.023$, so it is $9900\cdot (1+0.023)\cdot (1+0.023)$, which is $9900\cdot(1+0.023)^2$.

We can keep doing this until we get to $5$ years.

In conclusion, this is an expression for the town's population $5$ years from now:

$9900\cdot(1+0.023)^5$

assessment_items.question_content:
Benjamin invests $\$400$ in a savings account that earns $5\%$ interest each year.

**Which expression gives the balance in the account after $3$ years?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Growth at a rate of $5\%$ means the savings keep their $100\%$ and add $5\%$ more. So each year, the savings' balance is multiplied by $100\%+5\%$, which is the same as a factor of $(1+0.05)$.

After $0$ years (i.e., now), the balance is $400$ dollars.

After $1$ year, that number is multiplied by $1+0.05$, so it is $400\cdot (1+0.05)$.

After $2$ years, the number is again multiplied by $1+0.05$, so it is $400\cdot (1+0.05)\cdot (1+0.05)$, which is $400\cdot(1+0.05)^2$.

We can keep doing this until we get to $3$ years.

In conclusion, this is an expression for the balance in the account after $3$ years:

$400\cdot(1+0.05)\cdot(1+0.05)\cdot(1+0.05)$

assessment_items.question_content:
Fidel has a rare coin worth $\$550$. Each decade, the coin's value increases by $10\%$.

**Which expression gives the coin's value, $6$ decades from now?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Growth at a rate of $10\%$ means the coin's value keeps its $100\%$ and adds $10\%$ more. So each decade, the value is multiplied by $100\%+10\%$, which is the same as a factor of $(1+0.1)$.

After $0$ decades (i.e., now), the coin's value is $550$ dollars.

After $1$ decade, that number is multiplied by $1+0.1$, so it is $550\cdot (1+0.1)$.

After $2$ decades, the number is again multiplied by $1+0.1$, so it is $550\cdot (1+0.1)\cdot (1+0.1)$, which is $550\cdot(1+0.1)^2$.

We can keep doing this until we get to $6$ decades.

In conclusion, this is an expression for the coin's value, $6$ decades from now:

$550\cdot(1+0.1)^6$

assessment_items.question_content:
A manufacturing plant earned $\$80$ per a man-hour of labor when it opened. Each year, the plant earns an additional $6\%$ per man-hour.

**Which expression gives the amount the plant earned per a man-hour of labor $3$ years after it opened?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Growth at a rate of $6\%$ means the earnings keep their $100\%$ and add $6\%$ more. So each year, the earnings are multiplied by $100\%+6\%$, which is the same as a factor of $(1+0.06)$.

After $0$ years (i.e., when the plant opened), the earning was $80$ dollars per man-hour.

After $1$ year, that number is multiplied by $1+0.06$, so it is $80\cdot (1+0.06)$.

After $2$ years, the number is again multiplied by $1+0.06$, so it is $80\cdot (1+0.06)\cdot (1+0.06)$, which is $80\cdot(1+0.06)^2$.

We can keep doing this until we get to $3$ years.

In conclusion, this is an expression for the amount the plant earned per a man-hour of labor $3$ years after it opened:

$80\cdot(1+0.06)\cdot(1+0.06)\cdot(1+0.06)$

assessment_items.question_content:
Moore's law says that the number of transistors in a dense integrated circuit increases by $41\%$ every year. In $1974$, a dense integrated circuit was produced with $5000$ transistors.

**Which expression gives the number of transistors in a dense integrated circuit in $1979$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Growth at a rate of $41\%$ means the number of transistors keeps its $100\%$ and adds $41\%$ more. So each year, the number is multiplied by $100\%+41\%$, which is the same as a factor of $(1+0.41)$.

After $0$ years (i.e., in $1974$), the number of transistors in a dense integrated circuit was $5000$.

After $1$ year, that number was multiplied by $1+0.41$, so it was $5000\cdot (1+0.41)$.

After $2$ years, the number was again multiplied by $1+0.41$, so it was $5000\cdot (1+0.41)\cdot (1+0.41)$, which is $5000\cdot(1+0.41)^2$.

We can keep doing this until we get to $5$ years after $1974$, which is $1979$.

In conclusion, this is an expression for the number of transistors in a dense integrated circuit in $1979$:

$5000\cdot(1+0.41)^5$
